fatal_flaws: Incomplete novelty claim (truncated sentence)
Unclear integration of PRISMA 2020 with XAI/healthcare testing
fixable_flaws: Vague metric definition (consensus accuracy context)
Lack of comparison to existing benchmarking frameworks
reviewers.justification: While combining XAI with healthcare scenario testing shows promise, the current description lacks clarity in methodology and novelty completion, requiring refinement to demonstrate true innovation beyond existing XAI/healthcare literature.

fatal_flaws: No explicit baseline models/systems specified for comparison
fixable_flaws: Lack of details on synthetic data validation against real-world clinical datasets
reviewers.justification: While novel in combining XAI with dynamic healthcare testing, the absence of baseline comparisons and unclear synthetic data validation raise concerns about benchmark reliability.

fatal_flaws: PRISMA 2020 guidelines are designed for systematic reviews, not directly for evaluating AI reliability in dynamic scenarios
fixable_flaws: Novelty claim requires clearer differentiation from existing XAI frameworks
reviewers.justification: While the mechanism combines relevant concepts, the application of PRISMA 2020 to AI benchmarking requires stronger theoretical grounding. The integration of XAI principles with dynamic testing needs more explicit differentiation from prior work.

fatal_flaws: No details on synthetic data generation/validation process
No description of dynamic scenario implementation
fixable_flaws: Need expert panel selection/consensus protocol
Requires clarification of XAI integration methodology
reviewers.justification: While novel in concept, critical reproducibility elements like data generation and scenario implementation lack sufficient detail for replication.

fatal_flaws: Limited validation of synthetic data representativeness
fixable_flaws: Incomplete novelty differentiation from prior XAI frameworks
reviewers.justification: Promising framework but requires stronger validation of synthetic data and clearer novelty distinction

fixable_flaws: Lacks direct evidence of environmental volatility measurement methodology
Unclear comparison to existing dynamic evaluation frameworks
reviewers.justification: While the core concept is innovative, the provided evidence doesn't sufficiently support claims about environmental volatility handling or comparison to existing dynamic frameworks.

fatal_flaws: No baseline specification for comparison
Unclear integration of environmental variables in RL training
fixable_flaws: Lack of details on volatility measurement
No comparison to existing static frameworks in high-volatility scenarios
reviewers.justification: Critical lack of baseline specification and unclear environmental variable integration undermine experimental validity.

fatal_flaws: No direct evidence cited for reinforcement learning application in this context
Unclear link between digital transformation research and RL mechanism
fixable_flaws: Novelty claim requires stronger comparison to existing dynamic frameworks
Metric definition lacks statistical validation details
reviewers.justification: While the concept is promising, the mechanism's theoretical grounding requires stronger empirical support and clearer differentiation from existing approaches.

fatal_flaws: No implementation details or code provided
No experimental protocol or dataset description
fixable_flaws: Lacks baseline comparison specifics
No discussion of evaluation environment variability
reviewers.justification: Critical components for reproduction (implementation, protocol, data) are missing; insufficient detail to validate claims.

fatal_flaws: No empirical validation
Overly broad novelty claim without prior work analysis
fixable_flaws: Unclear reinforcement learning implementation details
Missing comparative baseline evaluation
reviewers.justification: Lacks empirical evidence and fails to substantiate novelty claim with prior research analysis required for conference-level submission.

fixable_flaws: Lack of direct evidence addressing clinical workflow integration challenges in cited references
reviewers.justification: While the application of context-aware attention mechanisms to clinical XAI appears novel, the provided evidence does not sufficiently demonstrate prior art exclusion in clinical workflow integration.

fatal_flaws: No baseline comparison with existing XAI methods in clinical context
fixable_flaws: Lack of quantitative metrics beyond subjective comprehension
No mention of real-world clinical validation
reviewers.justification: While the concept is novel, the absence of baseline comparisons with established XAI approaches in clinical settings undermines the experiment's validity and novelty claim.

fatal_flaws: Lack of clinical-specific attention mechanism design justification
No evidence linking attention to clinician comprehension metrics
fixable_flaws: Add clinical validation of attention relevance
Include comparative analysis with non-attention baselines
reviewers.justification: While innovative, the mechanism's clinical adaptation lacks theoretical grounding in provided evidence and requires empirical validation of attention's causal relationship with comprehension outcomes.

fatal_flaws: No primary studies or empirical validation provided to support claims
No details on simulation environment or clinician participant selection
fixable_flaws: Lack of algorithmic implementation specifics for attention mechanism
No description of clinical data stream characteristics used in experiments
reviewers.justification: While the concept is promising, critical reproducibility elements like experimental protocols and validation methodology remain unspecified. Direct evidence of meeting the comprehension metric in simulations is required.

fatal_flaws: Lacks direct clinical validation in real-world settings
No comparison to existing XAI methods in healthcare
fixable_flaws: Needs clearer differentiation from general XAI frameworks
Should address scalability for diverse clinical workflows
reviewers.justification: Promising novelty in context-aware XAI but requires empirical validation and comparative analysis against established methods.

fixable_flaws: Add direct competitor comparison and search query log.
required_experiments: Run novelty tribunal before any paper claim.

fixable_flaws: Add baseline and ablation before MVP claims.
required_experiments: Micro-probe with random baseline.
Probe with evidence-weighted baseline.

fixable_flaws: Tighten mechanism into falsifiable prediction.
required_experiments: Test whether the mechanism improves selection utility under controlled noise.

fixable_flaws: Record seed, command, environment, stdout, stderr, and metrics.
required_experiments: Run with fixed seed and persist config.

fixable_flaws: Position as workshop candidate until stronger empirical evidence exists.
required_experiments: Add venue-fit checklist after results.

fixable_flaws: Lacks explicit comparison to prior legal-AI comparative studies
PRISMA application to non-medical domains needs justification
reviewers.justification: While novel in applying systematic review methods to AI accountability laws, needs to better differentiate from existing framework comparison studies and clarify PRISMA's suitability for legal analysis.

fatal_flaws: Lacks explicit baseline jurisdiction for comparison
fixable_flaws: Needs clearer PRISMA adaptation justification for legal analysis
reviewers.justification: Novel approach but baseline jurisdiction definition is critical for conflict measurement

fatal_flaws: PRISMA guidelines are designed for systematic reviews of biomedical literature, not legal framework analysis - unproven adaptation
fixable_flaws: Lack of justification for selecting specific NLP techniques/legal ontologies
No discussion of jurisdictional variability in AI capability definitions
reviewers.justification: While novel in applying evidence synthesis to legal comparison, the mechanism's core methodology requires substantial justification for adapting PRISMA beyond biomedical contexts and addressing domain-specific challenges in legal text analysis.

fatal_flaws: No description of legal data sources or NLP preprocessing pipeline
No disclosure of jurisdiction selection criteria
fixable_flaws: Unclear conflict identification protocol
No inter-rater reliability metrics
reviewers.justification: While the framework concept is novel, critical methodological components required for reproduction are missing, particularly in legal data handling and conflict quantification.

fixable_flaws: Lacks technical depth in NLP/ontology implementation
Needs clearer jurisdictional scope definition
reviewers.justification: Novel application of evidence synthesis to legal AI accountability analysis aligns with interdisciplinary conference themes, though methodological details require strengthening.

fixable_flaws: Lacks direct evidence of bidirectional compatibility in healthcare context
reviewers.justification: While the concept is relevant to digital transformation, the provided evidence doesn't substantiate the novelty claim of bidirectional compatibility in healthcare legacy systems.

fatal_flaws: No baseline comparison against existing compatibility methods
Unclear evaluation of bidirectional compatibility claims
fixable_flaws: Lack of real-world testing beyond simulation
Insufficient detail on legacy system diversity
reviewers.justification: Critical lack of baseline comparisons and empirical validation in real healthcare IT environments undermines claims of efficacy and novelty.

fatal_flaws: Lack of direct evidence linking proposed mechanism to healthcare IT compatibility challenges
No prior work addressing bidirectional compatibility gap in references
fixable_flaws: Add domain-specific protocol examples
Include comparative analysis with existing adapter frameworks
reviewers.justification: While the concept is relevant, current evidence doesn't sufficiently justify novel bidirectional claims or healthcare-specific technical requirements.

fatal_flaws: No technical details on adapter architecture
No public dataset or simulation environment specifications
fixable_flaws: Missing comparison to existing compatibility methods
No error analysis on 5% failed transactions
reviewers.justification: Lacks sufficient technical details for implementation and validation; supporting evidence focuses on general concepts rather than reproducibility of specific claims.

fatal_flaws: Lack of direct evidence supporting technical claims
Cited references unrelated to legacy system compatibility
fixable_flaws: Add case studies or empirical validation
Include prior work on bidirectional compatibility
reviewers.justification: Submission lacks technical depth and relevant evidence for claimed novelty in legacy system adaptation.
